primary_sources.notes: Revenue ~₹19,000 Cr. Employee costs ~₹5,700 Cr (30%), Medical consumables ~₹5,300 Cr (28%), Power & utilities ~₹950 Cr (5%).
Revenue ~₹8,000 Cr. Employee costs ~31%, Medical consumables ~25%, Utilities ~5%.
Revenue ~₹5,500 Cr. Employee ~28%, Consumables ~27%, Utilities ~4.5%. Low-cost model with strong clinical outcomes.

primary_sources.source_name: NABH (National Accreditation Board for Hospitals) Quality Benchmarks 2024
primary_sources.notes: NABH accreditation standards include operational benchmarks for bed utilisation, nurse-patient ratios, and quality metrics.

primary_sources.source_name: National Health Authority (NHA) Hospital Cost Survey 2023-24
primary_sources.notes: NHA empanelled hospital cost structure data used for PM-JAY rate setting. Employee 25-35%, consumables 22-28%, overhead 15-20%.

source_metadata.methodology: P25/P50/P75 derived from FY24-25 annual reports of 3 listed India hospital chains (Apollo, Fortis, Narayana). NHA cost survey and NABH benchmarks used for cross-validation. Categories as % of net revenue (total hospital revenue). P25 reflects Narayana's low-cost high-efficiency model; P90 reflects high-cost quaternary facilities.

HR.source_note: Employee costs % of revenue. Narayana FY25: 28%, Apollo FY25: 30%, Fortis FY25: 31%. NHA survey: 25-35% range. Low-cost Indian hospital chains are at P25; quaternary/super-specialty at P75-P90.

OUTSOURCED.source_note: Medical consumables, implants, pharmaceuticals, blood products. Apollo FY25: 28%, Fortis FY25: 25%, Narayana: 27%. NHA: 22-28% range. High-end tertiary with complex surgery higher.

POWER_ENERGY.source_note: Power, utilities, medical gases. Apollo FY25: ~5%, Narayana: ~4.5%, Fortis: ~5%. NABH: energy intensity 4-7% of revenue typical.

FACILITIES.source_note: Rent, lease, occupancy. Apollo: lower (owns most properties). Fortis: higher (leased locations). New entrants / franchise model: 10-14%.

IT.source_note: Hospital information systems (HIS), EMR, radiology PACS, lab systems. Gartner Healthcare IT 2024: 2-4% of revenue. Telemedicine integration adds 1pp.

PLANT_MAINTENANCE.source_note: Medical equipment maintenance, biomedical engineering, building maintenance. NABH: hospitals spend 2-4% on maintenance; high-tech diagnostic centres higher.

MARKETING.source_note: Marketing, patient acquisition, OPD promotion. Apollo FY25: ~2.5% (brand + digital). Narayana: minimal marketing (referral model). Standalone multispecialty: 4-7%.

PROF_SVCS.source_note: Regulatory compliance, accreditation, legal, audit. NABH/NABL accreditation costs. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION.
